page: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine instance belonging to the DPDSv5 server family. Powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, it features 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, offering 1843 GB of temporary disk capacity, and the instance supports Premium Storage. Virtualization is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance scoring in the top 25% at 85110.68. Redis SET operations also demonstrate high efficiency, achieving 61.96 million operations per second. Single-core performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput fall within the average range. However, the instance shows weaker performance in text processing and large-scale LLM prompt processing, such as with 70B parameter models, which is a notable tradeoff of the Neoverse-N1 architecture.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D48pds_v5 leverages the ARM64 architecture to deliver high physical core density and dedicated resource allocation without hyperthreading overhead. This makes it a viable option for scale-out workloads that benefit from high core counts and local storage. The hardware profile is well-suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and multi-threaded file decompression tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine powered by a 48-core Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, alongside 1843 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core CPU performance and exceptional Redis SET operations, though it exhibits weaker results in text processing and large-scale LLM inference. The qualitative cost efficiency of its ARM architecture makes it a practical choice for scale-out workloads. It is highly suitable for database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v5 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Ampere Altra cores, 192 GB RAM, and 1843 GB local storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.

tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and local storage for balanced general-purpose and database workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARM64 cores powered by Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 at 3.0 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
1843 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high-throughput Redis SET operations
Optimized for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, and caching layers
